content:
# 2 Timothy Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2TI_001_page_001.jpg, 2TI_001_page_002.jpg

1. Paulos, an apostle of Christos Yeshua through [the] will of God, according to [the] promise of the life which [is] in Christos Yeshua,

2. to Timothy, [my] beloved child: Grace, mercy, peace from God [the] Father and Christos Yeshua our Lord.

3. I have gratitude to God, whom I serve from [my] forebears with a clean conscience, as I have unceasingly remembrance of you in my petitions, night and day,

4. longing to see you, having remembered your tears, that I may be filled with joy;

5. having received a reminder of the unhypocritical faith in you, which dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded [that it dwells] also in you.

6. Because of which I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.

7. For God did not give us a spirit of cowardice, but of power and of love and of self-discipline.

8. Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner, but suffer together [with me] for the gospel according to [the] power of God,

9. the [one] having saved us and having called [us] with a holy calling, not according to our works but according to [his] own purpose and grace, the [one] having been given to us in Christos Yeshua before times eternal,

10. but having now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior Christos Yeshua, who indeed abolished the death and brought to light life and incorruption through the gospel,

11. for which I was appointed a herald and an apostle and a teacher.

12. Because of which I also suffer these things; but I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to guard my deposit until that day.

13. Hold [the] pattern of sound words which you heard from me, in faith and love which [is] in Christos Yeshua.

14. The good deposit guard through [the] Holy Spirit, the [one] dwelling in us.

15. You know this, that all the [ones] in Asia turned away from me, among whom [are] Phygelus and Hermogenes.

16. May the Lord give mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain,

17. but being in Rome, he eagerly sought me and found [me].

18. May the Lord grant him to find mercy from [the] Lord in that day. And how much he served in Ephesus, you know very well.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.6 — "fan into flame":** anazōpyrein — "to rekindle/fan into flame." The gift (charisma) is not extinguished but needs stirring up. The image is of embers that still glow but need breath to blaze.

2. **v.7 — "a spirit of cowardice":** pneuma deilias — "a spirit of timidity/cowardice." The contrast: not cowardice but power, love, and self-discipline (sōphronismos). This triad governs Timothy's ministry.

3. **v.9 — "before times eternal":** pro chronōn aiōniōn — "before eternal times." God's saving purpose existed before time itself — predating creation and history.

4. **v.10 — "abolished the death":** katargēsantos ton thanaton — katargeō means "to render inoperative/nullify." Christ did not merely defeat death; he made it ineffective. The definite article (ton thanaton — "the death") treats death as a personified power.

5. **v.12 — "my deposit":** tēn parathēkēn mou — "my deposit/entrusted thing." Ambiguous: (a) what Paul has entrusted to God (his life/ministry), or (b) what God has entrusted to Paul (the gospel). The same term appears in v.14 ("the good deposit") where it clearly means the gospel.

— EMET, AI Translator
